Tests the `String.prototype.concat` method to verify it concatenates strings correctly.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test script that verifies the behavior of the `String.prototype.concat` method in JavaScript. It uses the `assert` function to check if concatenating the strings "a", "b", and "c" results in the string "abc". The test ensures that the `concat` method correctly joins multiple string arguments into a single string. This script provides narrow functionality, focusing specifically on validating the correct operation of the `concat` method for strings.
